summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held its regular meeting on February 3, 2026. The commission continued Zoning Case 2025-010 (Westbrook's Preliminary Development Plan major modification) to the next meeting at the applicant's request. The commission unanimously approved a text amendment (ZC 2026-001) initiated by the Board of Trustees to increase minimum lot sizes and setback requirements for residential PUD developments. The commission also voted to reschedule the March meeting from Tuesday, March 3rd to Monday, March 2nd to avoid a conflict with a community land use plan workshop.
decisions: Motion to continue Zoning Case 2025-010 (Westbrook's Preliminary Development Plan) to the March meeting passed (Curry-Yes, Dinwiddie-No, Lewis-Abstain, Pegram-Yes, Purcell-Yes)
Motion to recommend approval of text amendment ZC 2026-001 (residential PUD lot size and setback changes) passed unanimously (5-0)
Motion to reschedule March Zoning Commission meeting from Tuesday March 3rd to Monday March 2nd at 6:30 PM passed unanimously
Approval of transcribed meeting minutes from January 6, 2026 passed unanimously
action_items: Staff to publish notice of rescheduled March Zoning Commission meeting (Monday, March 2, 2026 at 6:30 PM)
Staff to prepare for continued hearing of Zoning Case 2025-010 at March 2nd meeting
Staff to forward recommendation of approval of text amendment ZC 2026-001 to the Board of Trustees
Mr. DeMarsh from planning commission to take steps on land use plan workshop scheduled for March 3rd
key_amounts: Minimum lot area for residential PUD use increased from 5,000 sq ft to 10,800 sq ft
Minimum lot width for residential PUD use: 50 feet
Minimum distance between rear facades of homes: 60 feet
Minimum distance between side facades of homes increased from 10 feet to 30 feet
Minimum side yard setback increased to 10 feet per property (15 feet standard)
Base zoning R-5 lot size is 12,000 sq ft; new PUD minimum of 10,800 sq ft is approximately 1,200 sq ft less
notable_topics: Continuance of Zoning Case 2025-010 Westbrook's Preliminary Development Plan major modification
Text amendment ZC 2026-001 to increase minimum lot sizes and setback requirements for residential PUD developments
Rescheduling March Zoning Commission meeting to avoid conflict with community land use plan workshop
Discussion of PUD residential lot standards including lot area, lot width, and facade separation distances
notes: Mr. Frisbee and Mr. Otkey were called during roll call but did not respond as present (apparently absent). The text amendment was initiated by the Board of Trustees rather than a private applicant, so staff served as both staff and applicant representative. The community land use plan workshop is scheduled for March 3rd and is open to the public with public comment opportunities.
title: ZC 2-3-26
